name: North Carolina Barbeque
recipeIngredient: Hot-pepper sauce for Seasoning at Table
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the roasts, garlic, and 3 cups of vinegar in a very large deep stockpot or flameproof casserole. If necessary, divide the ingredients between 2 smaller pots.
2. Add water to cover by at least 2 inches and bring to a boil over high heat.
3. Reduce the heat, partially cover, and simmer for 2-1/2 hours, adding boiling water as needed to keep the meat covered.
4. Remove the roasts from the pot, reserving the cooked garlic cloves and 1/2 cup of pork-simmering liquid. Place the roasts on a rack in a large roasting pan.
5. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
6. Meanwhile, prepare the Mopping Sauce by mashing the cooked garlic to a paste with salt in a large bowl.
7. Add reserved pork-simmering liquid, sugar, cayenne, pepper, and vinegar, then mix well.
8. Remove 1-1/2 cups of the Mopping Sauce and set aside for Mixing Sauce; use the remaining sauce to baste the pork as it roasts.
9. Baste the roasts with Mopping Sauce and place them in a 350-degree oven. Reduce the heat to 300 degrees.
10. Cook for 2 hours, basting every 15 minutes and turning once or twice.
11. Use all of the Mopping Sauce (none of the reserved sauce).
12. Remove the pork from the oven and transfer to a large chopping board. Discard bones and remove any fat.
13. Chop/shred the lean pork fairly fine. Transfer to a bowl and cover loosely to keep warm and moist.
14. Place the empty, unwashed roasting pan over very low heat and add 1/2 cup of reserved Mopping Sauce.
15. Scrape the pan to loosen caramelized juices and browned bits, stirring quickly and constantly.
16. Pour every last drop back into the remaining reserved Mopping Sauce.
17. Add the reserved pork-simmering liquid and stir in the smoky-flavored barbecue sauce. Season with salt to taste.
18. Let the Mixing Sauce mellow considerably as the barbecue ripens. Add half the sauce to the chopped pork and mix well.
19. Gradually blend in the remaining sauce. Let cool, cover tightly, and refrigerate for at least 12 hours.
20. Chopped barbecue can be prepared in advance and refrigerated for up to 2 days or frozen for up to 2 months.
21. Before serving, let return to room temperature, then reheat covered in a microwave oven or 300-degree conventional oven. Adjust seasonings as necessary and serve hot with cole slaw and hot-pepper sauce.
